original_image_tags: A scanned document page, appearing to be the reverse side of a document or a slip. The page is mostly blank with two hole-punch circles at the top margin. Faint text bleed-through from the reverse side is visible but illegible. The bottom edge features several blue ink date/time stamps indicating routing through FBI offices (Belmont, Sullivan, Domestic Intelligence Division) in late April 1964.

elements.description: Multiple blue ink stamps at the bottom reading 'REC'D DOM INTELL DIV', 'APR 27 12:50 PM '64', 'REC'D SULLIVAN FBI - JUSTICE APR 27 12:17 PM '64', and 'REC'D BELMONT FBI JUSTICE APR 27 10:45 AM '64'. A faint red ink stamp at the bottom left reads 'APR 28 1964'. The rest of the page is blank with two punch holes at the top.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content (stamp/text content and punch holes), but Description B adds specific quoted text for stamps that Description A only summarizes, without any contradiction.
